title: [openai] Remove incorrect tool call id from tool call delta
body:
For OpenAI responses API, the `response.function_call_arguments.delta` returns an `item_id` [(docs)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses-streaming/response/function_call_arguments/delta) . This item_id is actually the `id` of the tool_call, rather than the `tool_call_id`. 

The problem is that when using agent_stream or more generally the parts_manager, these deltas are accumulated and the `tool_call_id` for the ToolCallNode ends up being set to the incorrect `id`. This is particularly problematic because the `id` is a longer string than is supported by some gpt models (for example, an id is `fc_6876aca7db8c8192af95414c6cf92796035a7a219e794395`, whereas the tool call id for the same request is `call_YHzL9zYTlauY9bw9dEoE4uA4`. 

`gpt-4o-mini` only allows tool call ids that are 40 characters or less, so using the longer id breaks things (and is incorrect from a tool call id perspective).

files_modified.patch:
@@ -1051,7 +1051,7 @@ async def _get_event_iterator(self) -> AsyncIterator[ModelResponseStreamEvent]:
                     vendor_part_id=chunk.item_id,
                     tool_name=None,
                     args=chunk.delta,
-                    tool_call_id=chunk.item_id,
+                    tool_call_id=None,
                 )
                 if maybe_event is not None:  # pragma: no branch
                     yield maybe_event

steps.operation: Stop propagating the Responses API `item_id` into `ToolCallPart.tool_call_id` when building streamed tool-call-argument delta parts
steps.reason: In OpenAI Responses streaming, `response.function_call_arguments.delta.item_id` corresponds to the tool call's internal `id` (often long, e.g. `fc_...`), not the `tool_call_id` (e.g. `call_...`). Accumulating deltas via `agent_stream`/`parts_manager` was incorrectly setting `ToolCallNode.tool_call_id` to this long `id`, which can violate model constraints (e.g. `gpt-4o-mini` requires tool call ids <= 40 chars) and is semantically wrong. Setting `tool_call_id=None` prevents poisoning the accumulated tool call id from deltas.
steps.side_effects: Any downstream logic that previously (incorrectly) relied on delta events carrying `tool_call_id` will no longer see it populated for these delta parts; consumers should instead derive the correct tool call id from the proper tool call start/metadata events. This change should reduce failures when models enforce tool-call-id length/format constraints and may slightly change how parts are correlated if code used `tool_call_id` rather than `vendor_part_id`/other identifiers.

step_plan.summary: Fixes OpenAI Responses streaming handling by removing the incorrect assignment of `item_id` to `tool_call_id` in function-call-arguments delta events, preventing invalid/overlong tool call ids from being accumulated.
